Sebastian Peñaherrera

Sebastian Peñaherrera is a biotechnology engineer, researcher, and entrepreneur focused on curing age-related diseases and extending healthy human lifespan.

His work sits at the intersection of regenerative medicine, mitochondrial biology, translational bioengineering, and precision health. He has conducted research on mitochondrial transfer, stem cell-derived therapies, senescence reduction, wound healing, extracellular mitochondrial DNA, and long non-coding RNAs relevant to skin metabolism and regenerative therapies.

He served as Director’s Research Assistant at the Biomedical Institute at Universidad San Francisco de Quito (USFQ), where he worked on in vitro 3D culture systems that better mimic human physiology and improve the relevance of preclinical testing. He designed and tested a protocol that reduced senescence markers by 36% in induced senescent fibroblasts.

Sebastian has authored peer-reviewed publications in regenerative medicine and mitochondrial science, contributed to international scientific collaborations, and is co-owner of a submitted patent related to conditioned media that reduces senescence and enhances fibroblast proliferation.

Beyond the lab, he founded a biotechnology startup and worked on turning high-effort scientific work into practical health interventions with real-world impact.

Contact
Email: sebastian.penaherrera.c@gmail.com
Location: Quito, Ecuador
